title: centaurea solstitialis subsp. carneola
intro: Centaurea solstitialis subsp. carneola, also known as the prickly lettuce or artichoke thistle, is a subspecies of the Centaurea solstitialis plant, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Centaurea solstitialis subsp. carneola include soothing digestive issues, alleviating respiratory problems, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and other women's health issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
